title: United States orders suspension of access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for foreign users
summary: The US government has forced Anthropic to deactivate its most powerful AI models to comply with foreign access regulations.

title: Meta employees criticize the atmosphere in the AI unit and Zuckerberg acknowledges mistakes
summary: Engineers describe Meta's AI unit as a 'gulag' and Zuckerberg's hackathon plan generates rejection, while he admits failures in the restructuring.

title: Various news about the World Cup: Haiti shirt, New York campaign, tickets and schedule
summary: Includes controversy over the Haitian shirt design, the New York advertising campaign, the increase in ticket prices, and tips for watching the matches.

title: Highlighted tech deals: Office 2024, Smart TV, Starlink and more
summary: Discounts on Office licenses, Samsung TVs, MediaMarkt products, and reduced-price Starlink connection.

title: Courts and governments act against artificial intelligence abuses: Google liable, Chinese scams and Conan O'Brien
summary: Google is held responsible for false statements by its AI, a Chinese AI scam operation is uncovered, and Conan O'Brien joins the fight against these frauds.

title: New research links meditation, supplements and cancer treatments to cognition and longevity
summary: Studies show that meditation improves cognitive performance, a common supplement could shorten life, and a treatment for pancreatic tumors reveals a possible cancer switch.

title: News and tips for Android phones: blue dot, AirDrop compatibility, VoLTE and more
summary: Includes explanations about the privacy indicator on Android, how to activate VoLTE, Motorola's uncopied feature, and eSIM tricks.

title: SpaceX closes its IPO with a 19% increase and makes Elon Musk a trillionaire
summary: SpaceX's IPO marks a financial milestone by reaching a record valuation and generating the first trillion-dollar fortune.

title: Arch Linux contains a malware attack that compromised more than 1500 packages
summary: The Arch Linux distribution manages to contain a security incident in which AUR packages were infected with malicious software and rootkits.

title: Jeff Bezos founds an AI company to create 'artificial engineers' and raises $12 billion
summary: The Amazon founder invests in generative artificial intelligence to automate software engineering tasks.

title: Apple prepares iPhone 18 Pro with new aluminum and reveals hints about its AI strategy
summary: The next iPhone could be made with a more resistant and cheaper process, while the company hints at its AI direction.

title: United States bans differential privacy in the census and FBI builds a simulated town for cyberattacks
summary: The US government takes measures in census data collection and in preparation against cyberattacks through realistic simulations.

title: Mastodon releases its 4.6 candidate and the debate on open source in AI grows
summary: The social network Mastodon publishes its candidate version with new features, while the discussion on the importance of open source in AI intensifies.

title: Video game and hardware news: Steam Machine, GTA VI, GPU and indie games
summary: Valve sends Steam Machine units, speculation about gas in GTA VI, RTX 5080/3090 tested, and new games like Shepherd's Dog presented.

title: News in open source software and tools: GLM 5.2, Meta Edits, archived tools
summary: Release of GLM 5.2, Meta adds AI assistant to Edits, open source project archives after funding, and developer leaves Mozilla.

title: Business and startup news: OpenAI under investigation, Microsoft considers spinning off Xbox, Andrew Yang proposes reducing cost of living
summary: OpenAI faces state investigation, Microsoft considers spinning off Xbox, Andrew Yang sees opportunity in lowering cost of living, and SpaceX hints at merger with Tesla.

title: Consumer and technology news in Spain: openings, prices and hardware
summary: Includes opening of a new shopping center in Zaragoza, changes in tobacco prices, opera broadcast, new LG monitor, 6G roadmap, and AMD warranty.

title: Science and lifestyle articles: science, history, work and consumer trends
summary: Articles from Xataka and Fast Company on eclipses, digestive myths, work productivity, animal welfare, couple happiness and more.

title: Technical and cybersecurity news: retro hardware, vulnerabilities, programming and sustainability
summary: From running DOS on old hardware to critical flaws in Splunk, through AI analysis of medical bills and low-power computing.

summary: The latest tools and platforms for developers and creators debut on Product Hunt.
